title: Red Sea rerouting to impact Suezmaxes || US Gulf STS recovering
section_path: Red Sea rerouting to impact Suezmaxes || US Gulf STS recovering
section_path_text: Red Sea rerouting to impact Suezmaxes || US Gulf STS recovering
summary:
Main
This week, in the East we focus on Suezmaxes heading to Europe and LRs carrying naphtha to Asia in the context of Red Sea developments. In the West, we discuss the resumption of STS in the US Gulf after recent cold weather, and on the clean side we examine why MR rates out of UK Cont have skyrocketed. By Mary Melton

ANALYSIS / EAST OF SUEZ / DIRTY


Red Sea rerouting to significantly impact global Suezmax tonne-miles
Suezmax monthly average tonne-miles by route and with projected increase via Cape of Good Hope
Middle East-origin crude flows to Europe have all now started rerouting to Europe via the Cape of Good Hope (COGH), though loadings do appear to be continuing normally.

children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2024-01-26T114551.208.png | assets/2024-01-26_red-sea-rerouting-to-impact-suezmaxes-us-gulf-sts-recovering_img_unnamed-2024-01-26t114551208_c45787aaff21.png
Suezmax monthly average tonne-miles by route and with projected increase via Cape of Good Hope
Middle East-origin crude flows to Europe have all now started rerouting to Europe via the Cape of Good Hope (COGH), though loadings do appear to be continuing normally. Only 2 transits have been observed via the Bab el-Mandeb since US-led strikes began on 12th January, the last of which occurred 6 days ago.

children.title: USG STS operations starting to recover after recent cold snap
section_path: USG STS operations starting to recover after recent cold snap
children.section_path_text: USG STS operations starting to recover after recent cold snap
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2024-01-26T114802.432.png | assets/2024-01-26_red-sea-rerouting-to-impact-suezmaxes-us-gulf-sts-recovering_img_unnamed-2024-01-26t114802432_1e32c4521da3.png
US Gulf Coast STS movements by origin port
STS operations in the US Gulf Coast were hampered last week with the onset of a cold snap which hit 13th January and led to rough sea conditions and port closures. Houston was the port most impacted by this weather event, with crude STS loadings falling to 575kbd last week, an almost 44% decrease compared to December loadings of 1mbd.
